# Feature: Environments Pane Actions

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-005  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_pane_actions.md`

Add per-environment context menu actions (Rename and Delete) in the Environments pane with the same interaction standards used by other panes: iconized dropdown actions, red destructive styling for delete, confirm-before-delete behavior, adapter-backed persistence, and uniqueness-validated inline rename.

This feature delivers consistency across sidebar panes and reduces accidental destructive edits while keeping the environment workflow fast and predictable.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

`.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature uses existing environment domain models and UI state; no new persisted entities are introduced.

### Entities & Attributes

- **`Environment`** (existing persisted model)
  - `id` (string) - stable environment identifier used for selection, rename, and delete.
  - `name` (string) - display name and uniqueness key for list rows.
  - `values` (`EnvironmentVariable[]`) - environment variable entries shown elsewhere (count shown in row badge).

- **`EnvironmentsSlice`** (existing Zustand slice)
  - `environments` (`Environment[]`) - source list rendered by `EnvironmentsPane`.
  - `activeEnvironment` (`Environment | null`) - currently selected environment.
  - `updateEnvironment(id, updates)` - updates list + active environment projection after successful rename persistence.
  - `removeEnvironment(id)` - removes deleted environment and clears `activeEnvironment` when the deleted row was active.
  - `addEnvironment(environment)` - existing case-insensitive uniqueness behavior used as baseline rule for rename validation.

- **`EnvironmentsPane` local UI state** (existing + added)
  - Existing: `isImportWizardOpen`, `isAddWizardOpen`.
  - New: `editingEnvironmentId` (`string | null`) and `editingName` (string) for inline rename lifecycle.

- **`useConfirmDialog` state** (existing shared hook from FEAT-001)
  - `isOpen` (boolean), `isConfirming` (boolean), and action-specific `options` for delete confirmation.

### Relationships

- `EnvironmentsSlice.environments` 1:N rows rendered by `EnvironmentsPane`.
- `activeEnvironment` references a single `Environment` by `id`; `removeEnvironment` ensures this relationship is cleared when the target is deleted.
- One confirm-dialog invocation maps to one environment delete target (`id` + `name`).

### Indexes & Constraints

- Environment names must remain unique case-insensitively (same semantic rule as `addEnvironment`).
- Rename persistence must flow through adapter boundary (`saveEnvironment`) before store mutation is finalized.
- Delete must be explicit-confirm only: adapter call occurs only after user confirmation.

---

## 3. Files

List of files this feature is expected to create or modify:

- `packages/core/src/components/common/EnvironmentsPane.tsx` - add row dropdown menu actions, inline rename flow, and confirm-before-delete behavior.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/EnvironmentsPane.test.tsx` - component tests for FEAT-005 rename/delete behavior and regressions.

---

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001**: Shared confirmation hook `useConfirmDialog` must remain available for delete confirmation UX.
- Existing storage adapter methods: `saveEnvironment(environment)` and `deleteEnvironment(environmentId)`.
- Existing store actions/selectors: `updateEnvironment`, `removeEnvironment`, `addEnvironment`, `setActiveEnvironment` (if needed by selection flow).

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add per-row Environments dropdown actions and inline rename workflow

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `EnvironmentsPane.tsx` row rendering to include a hover-visible `DropdownMenu` trigger (three-dots icon) with two actions: Rename (`PencilIcon`) and Delete (`Trash2Icon`, red class). Add local rename state (`editingEnvironmentId`, `editingName`) and inline input behavior matching other panes (Enter commit, Escape cancel, blur commit, stop propagation on input/menu interactions so row-click selection is preserved).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Create tests that verify (1) each row renders Rename/Delete actions, (2) Rename opens inline input seeded with current name, (3) Enter commits rename, (4) Escape cancels rename without persistence call.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add/refresh component-level JSDoc and concise inline comments in `EnvironmentsPane.tsx` documenting row action behavior and event-propagation safeguards.
- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- EnvironmentsPane` and manually confirm row click still selects an environment while menu actions remain independently clickable.

### TASK-002: Enforce rename uniqueness and adapter-backed persistence for environment renames

- [ ] **Implementation**: Implement rename commit logic in `EnvironmentsPane.tsx` that trims input, validates case-insensitive uniqueness across all other environments, shows `notification.showNotification('error', ...)` on duplicates, and persists valid renames with `storageAdapter.saveEnvironment(updatedEnvironment)`. Update store via `updateEnvironment(id, { name: trimmedName })` only after successful adapter result; show error notification on adapter failure.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add tests that verify (1) duplicate rename is rejected with error notification and no adapter save call, (2) successful rename calls `saveEnvironment` and updates rendered name/store-backed row, (3) adapter failure surfaces error and leaves original name unchanged.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Document uniqueness rules and adapter/store update ordering with targeted comments near rename commit logic.
- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- EnvironmentsPane` and manually attempt duplicate and unique renames to confirm expected notifications and persisted results.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001.

### TASK-003: Add confirm-before-delete for environments and FEAT-005 regression coverage

- [ ] **Implementation**: Integrate `useConfirmDialog` in `EnvironmentsPane.tsx` and route Delete action through `openConfirmDialog(...)` with destructive copy. In confirm callback, call `storageAdapter.deleteEnvironment(environment.id)`; on success call `removeEnvironment(environment.id)` and show success notification; on failure show error notification and throw to keep confirm lifecycle consistent with other panes.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Add tests that verify (1) clicking Delete opens dialog with target environment name, (2) cancel does not call adapter delete, (3) confirm calls adapter delete and removes the row, (4) adapter delete failure shows error and keeps row intact, (5) delete action retains destructive red styling.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add file-level FEAT-005 regression docblock in `EnvironmentsPane.test.tsx` and update delete-handler comments in `EnvironmentsPane.tsx` describing confirm-gated destructive flow.
- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- EnvironmentsPane` and manually confirm deletion always requires explicit confirmation.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: All adapter operations follow the `Result` pattern; errors are surfaced with `notification.showNotification('error', message)` and must not mutate store state on failure.
- **Logging**: No new logging pipeline required; rely on existing adapter/service logging conventions for storage failures.

---

- [x] TASK-002: Enforce rename uniqueness and adapter-backed persistence for environment renames
